proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Presence of brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Greater weight loss in the prior 6 months (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Each 1-unit increase in ECOG PS reduces PFS by 1.18 months (p<1e-300).
Stage IV disease reduces PFS by 1.54 months on average (p<1e-300).
Brain metastases reduce PFS by 1.05 months (p=1.8e-179).
Each g/dL of albumin increases PFS by 0.50 months (p=9.4e-112).
Each 1% body-weight loss reduces PFS by 0.075 months (p=4.1e-148).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (consistent with worse functional reserve).
ER-positive (er_positive=1) tumors have longer pfs_months than ER-negative tumors.
HER2-positive (her2_positive=1) tumors have shorter pfs_months than HER2-negative tumors (more aggressive biology).
PIK3CA-mutated tumors (pik3ca_mutation=1) have shorter pfs_months than PIK3CA wild-type tumors.
Higher Ki-67 proliferation index (ki67_pct) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Each 1-year increase in age increases PFS by 0.176 months — direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis (older patients have longer PFS in this cohort, p<1e-300).
ER+ tumors have +0.57 months PFS vs ER- (p=2.2e-121).
HER2+ tumors have -0.46 months PFS vs HER2- (p=1.2e-56).
PIK3CA-mutated tumors have -0.56 months PFS vs WT (p=2.4e-126).
Each 1% increase in Ki-67 reduces PFS by 0.019 months (p=3.0e-93).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_tamoxifen is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_trastuzumab is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months overall.

analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib-treated patients have mean PFS 5.41 vs 4.31 untreated (+1.10 months, p<1e-300).
Tamoxifen-treated +0.043 months vs untreated, not statistically significant (p=0.077).
Trastuzumab-treated -0.060 months vs untreated, marginal (p=0.030); direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis.
Olaparib-treated -0.036 months, not significant (p=0.33).
Sacituzumab govitecan -0.007 months, not significant (p=0.84).
Pembrolizumab -0.074 months, marginally significant in negative direction (p=0.017); direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment assignment is roughly random with respect to clinical/biomarker features (synthetic dataset; no confounding by indication).

analyses.code: for tx in treatments: prevalence within ER+ vs ER-, HER2+ vs HER2-, BRCA+ vs WT
analyses.result_summary: Across all six treatments, prevalence within biomarker subgroups differs from overall by <1%. Treatments are essentially randomly assigned, so unadjusted main effects estimate causal effect under random allocation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months specifically in ER-positive (er_positive=1) patients (canonical indication).

analyses.result_summary: Within ER+ patients, tamoxifen mean 4.88 vs 4.86 (diff +0.023, p=0.45). No detectable benefit; refutes h5.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months specifically in HER2-positive (her2_positive=1) patients (canonical indication).

analyses.result_summary: Within HER2+ patients, trastuzumab mean 4.25 vs 4.33 (diff -0.08, p=0.15). No benefit; direction slightly negative; refutes h6.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib prolongs pfs_months specifically in BRCA1- or BRCA2-mutated patients (brca_any=1).

analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA1/2-mutated patients (n=2499), olaparib mean 4.93 vs 4.59 (diff +0.346, p=0.040). Modest unadjusted benefit consistent with h7.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan prolongs pfs_months specifically in triple-negative (er=pr=her2=0) patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months specifically in triple-negative (er=pr=her2=0) patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within TNBC (n=10496), sacituzumab mean 4.23 vs 4.31 (diff -0.085, p=0.21). No benefit; refutes h8.1.
Within TNBC (n=10496), pembrolizumab mean 4.37 vs 4.29 (diff +0.072, p=0.21). Trend positive but not significant overall.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib's PFS benefit is concentrated in HR-positive HER2-negative tumors (the canonical CDK4/6-inhibitor indication).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ palbo*hr_pos_her2_neg with main effects; subgroup means
analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib x HR+/HER2- interaction: +1.82 months (p<1e-300). Within HR+/HER2-: palbo +1.81 months. Outside HR+/HER2-: palbo -0.009 months (NS). Strongly supports h9.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within HR+/HER2- patients, treatment_palbociclib's PFS benefit is suppressed in PIK3CA-mutated tumors (PIK3CA mutations confer resistance to CDK4/6 inhibition).

analyses.result_summary: Palbo x PIK3CA interaction within HR+/HER2-: -2.73 months (p<1e-300). HR+/HER2- AND PIK3CA WT: palbo +2.74 months; HR+/HER2- AND PIK3CA mutated: palbo +0.015 (NS). PIK3CA mutation entirely abrogates palbociclib benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT patients, treatment_palbociclib's benefit further depends on ER positivity (ER+ vs PR-only/HR+).

analyses.code: OLS within HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA WT: pfs ~ palbo*er_positive
analyses.result_summary: Within HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA WT, palbo x er_positive interaction: +2.82 months (p=6e-71). ER+ gain: +2.91; ER- (PR-only) gain: +0.09 (NS). Palbociclib benefit is specifically in ER+ tumors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT tumors, treatment_palbociclib's benefit depends inversely on Ki-67 proliferation index, with effect attenuating sharply above some threshold.

analyses.code: OLS within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA WT: pfs ~ palbo*ki67_pct; binned ki67 thresholds
analyses.result_summary: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA WT (n=18681), palbo*ki67 interaction: -0.135/unit ki67 (p<1e-300). Threshold: at ki67<=14, palbo effect ~+4.94 months; at ki67>14, palbo effect ~0. Sharp cutoff at ki67=14.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete palbociclib responsive subgroup is: er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<=14, in which palbociclib increases pfs_months by ~5 months; outside this subgroup it has no effect.

analyses.code: Within subgroup: OLS pfs ~ palbo (and adjusted for prognostic covars). Outside subgroup: OLS pfs ~ palbo.
analyses.result_summary: In subgroup (n=10828): palbociclib +4.94 months unadjusted, +4.98 months adjusted for age/ECOG/stage/brain mets/albumin/weight loss (p<1e-300). Outside subgroup: palbociclib effect +0.007 (p=0.75). Joint OLS interaction term: +4.94 (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Trastuzumab x feature interaction screen will reveal a subgroup (e.g. HER2+ AND another modifier) in which trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months.

analyses.code: For each feature f: OLS pfs ~ trastuzumab + f + trastuzumab*f
analyses.result_summary: Strongest trastuzumab interaction overall: tumor_size_cm (+0.037/cm, p=0.018). Within HER2+: ldh_u_l (-0.0018/U, p=0.007) — implies trastuzumab benefit only at low LDH; even at LDH=100, gain is only +0.15 months and not robust. After adjusting for prognostic covariates, no subgroup shows reliable trastuzumab benefit. Effects are below the noise floor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib x feature interaction screen will identify the BRCA-mutated subgroup as the responsive subgroup.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ olaparib + brca_any + olaparib*brca_any; further nesting
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib x BRCA(any) interaction: +0.40 months (p=0.021). Sub-stratification: BRCA+/HR-: +0.83 months (p=0.011); BRCA+/HR+: +0.19 (NS). After adjustment for age/ECOG/stage/brain mets/albumin/weight loss, the within-BRCA+/HR- olaparib coefficient drops to -0.003 (p=0.94) — the unadjusted +0.83 is largely driven by chance baseline imbalance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab x feature interaction screen will reveal a heterogeneity pattern, plausibly concentrating any benefit in TNBC, low-burden, premenopausal, or low-Ki-67 immunologically active subgroups.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ pembro*X for X in features; deeper subgroup probing within TNBC
analyses.result_summary: Strongest interaction terms: pembro*triple_neg = +0.185 (p=0.015), pembro*postmenopausal in TNBC = -0.334 (p=0.005), pembro*ki67_pct in TNBC = -0.011/unit (p=0.017). Direction: any pembro benefit concentrates in TNBC + premenopausal + low Ki-67.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months in the joint subgroup triple_neg=1 AND postmenopausal=0 AND ki67_pct<=14, with no benefit (and possibly harm) outside.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ pembro within and outside subgroup, then with adjustment for age/ECOG/stage/brain mets/albumin/weight loss
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted: subgroup (n=2388) pembro +0.54 months (p=2e-5); outside subgroup pembro -0.10 (p=0.001). After adjustment for prognostic covariates: subgroup pembro coef = +0.016 (p=0.12); outside subgroup pembro coef = -0.038 (p=0.03). Apparent within-subgroup benefit collapses on adjustment, indicating chance imbalance — no real pembrolizumab effect once prognostic factors are accounted for.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sacituzumab govitecan prolongs pfs_months in some subgroup of TNBC or HR+/HER2- (the two FDA-approved indications).

analyses.code: Subgroup means: TNBC, HR+/HER2-, and stratifications by ECOG/stage/ki67/menopausal
analyses.result_summary: TNBC: -0.085 (p=0.21); HR+/HER2-: 0.000 (p=1.0); TNBC + premenopausal: -0.28 (p=0.009) — direction OPPOSITE to indication; HR+/HER2- + brain mets: +0.40 (p=0.013) but biologically implausible and likely chance. No coherent positive subgroup; refutes h18.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tamoxifen has a small ER+ specific effect on pfs_months, possibly seen in low-Ki-67 ER+ subset.

analyses.code: ER+ subgroup means; ER+ x premenopausal; ER+ x ki67<14
analyses.result_summary: ER+: +0.023 (p=0.45). ER+ HER2-: +0.025 (p=0.48). ER+ HER2- premeno: +0.047 (p=0.40). ER+ ki67<14: -0.002 (p=0.97). No tamoxifen effect detected in any natural subgroup; refutes h19.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive 2-feature subgroup search across all binary feature pairs will not surface any treatment-effect subgroup with effect size approaching the palbociclib subgroup, for any of the other five treatments.

analyses.code: For each tx and each pair of binary features (f1=v1 & f2=v2): compute within-subgroup tx effect; sort by effect size
analyses.result_summary: Top per-treatment 2-feature subgroup effects (unadjusted): trastuzumab +0.54 (BRCA1+/TNBC, n=53 treated); olaparib +0.87 (BRCA1+/node+); pembrolizumab +0.39; sacituzumab +0.40; tamoxifen +0.43. All are 5-10x smaller than the palbociclib subgroup effect (+4.94), and most rest on small denominators or fail adjustment for prognostic factors. Confirms h20.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Trastuzumab in HER2+ shows no benefit even after stratification on stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ECOG, ki67, LDH, or node status.

analyses.code: Stratified means in HER2+ across multiple modifiers
analyses.result_summary: HER2+ node+: -0.09 (p=0.24); HER2+ node-: -0.06 (p=0.40); HER2+ stage IV: -0.11 (p=0.24); HER2+ not stage IV: -0.04 (p=0.51); HER2+ no brain mets ECOG<=1: -0.08 (p=0.19); HER2+ ki67<14: -0.05 (p=0.45); HER2+ low LDH (<200): -0.03 (p=0.72). No subgroup shows trastuzumab benefit. Confirms h21.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab's positive interaction in TNBC + premenopausal + low Ki-67 reflects chance imbalance in baseline prognostic factors among a small treated subgroup, not a true treatment effect.

analyses.code: Compare baseline covariates (age, ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin, weight_loss) between pembro+ and pembro- in subgroup; refit pembro effect adjusted for covariates
analyses.result_summary: In TNBC+premeno+ki67<=14, pembro+ patients are older (66.3 vs 64.5), less likely stage IV (26% vs 32%), less brain mets (8.8% vs 11%), less weight loss (3.48 vs 3.94). Adjusting for these, pembro coefficient drops from +0.54 (p=2e-5) to +0.016 (p=0.12). Supports h22.1: subgroup signal is largely confounded by chance baseline imbalance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib in BRCA-mutant HR-negative tumors does NOT confer a real PFS benefit once age, ECOG, stage_iv, brain mets, albumin, and weight_loss are adjusted for; the unadjusted +0.83 is chance.

analyses.code: Within BRCA+ HR- (n=629): OLS pfs ~ olaparib + age + ecog + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin + weight_loss
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib coefficient: -0.003 (p=0.94). Unadjusted +0.83 disappears with covariate adjustment; the mutated/HR- subgroup is small (46 olaparib-treated) and the unadjusted contrast is dominated by random imbalance. Supports h23.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable joint model containing all six treatments (with palbociclib x [ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA WT/ki67<=14] subgroup interaction) and the strongest prognostic features (age, ECOG, stage_iv, brain mets, albumin, weight_loss, Ki-67) will explain almost all PFS variance, with the palbociclib-subgroup interaction the only meaningful treatment-effect term.

analyses.code: Single OLS with all six treatments, palbo*subgroup, pembro*subgroup, ola*subgroup, and prognostic covars
analyses.result_summary: Joint model R^2 = 0.994. Significant terms: palbo*subgroup +4.977 (p<1e-300); age +0.177/yr; ecog -1.170/grade; stage_iv -1.557; has_brain_mets -0.971; albumin +0.488/g/dL; weight_loss -0.078/%. Other treatment main and interaction terms all near 0 and not meaningful. Confirms h24.1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final best-supported treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis: treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months by approximately 4.94-4.98 months in the joint subgroup defined by er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<=14, with no detectable effect in any other subgroup. PIK3CA mutation, HER2 positivity, ER negativity, and high Ki-67 (>14%) each independently abolish palbociclib benefit.

For the remaining five treatments (tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab govitecan, pembrolizumab), no subgroup with a robust (covariate-adjusted) PFS benefit could be identified in this 50,000-patient cohort. Apparent unadjusted benefits from olaparib in BRCA+/HR- and pembrolizumab in TNBC+premenopausal+low-Ki-67 do not survive adjustment for the strong prognostic covariates (age, ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo).

analyses.code: Joint OLS pfs ~ palbo + palbo:subgroup + prognostic covars; subgroup verification by within-stratum means
analyses.result_summary: Final palbociclib subgroup interaction: +4.977 months (p<1e-300, adjusted). Within-subgroup mean PFS: 9.23 (palbo+) vs 4.29 (palbo-) at low Ki-67 quartiles. The subgroup contains 21.7% of the cohort (n=10,828). Outside, palbo effect = -0.0005 (p=0.80).

analyses.code: Adjusted within-subgroup OLS for olaparib (BRCA+/HR-) and pembrolizumab (TNBC+premeno+ki67<=14)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib in BRCA+/HR-: -0.003 (p=0.94). Adjusted pembrolizumab in TNBC+premeno+ki67<=14: +0.016 (p=0.12). Outside-subgroup pembrolizumab: -0.038 (p=0.03). Tamoxifen, trastuzumab, sacituzumab show no subgroup benefit at any adjustment level. Confirms h25.2.
